chunk_summary: Guidelines for preventive supplementation doses in high-risk vegetarian diets, emphasizing vitamin B12, iron, vitamin A, vitamin D, calcium, and zinc, tailored to national schedules and specific risk factors.
learning_objectives: Understand the preventive supplementation needs for high-risk vegetarian patterns, including vegan diets and dairy-poor diets.
Learn the recommended dosages for vitamin B12, iron, vitamin A, vitamin D, calcium, and zinc in children and adolescents.
Recognize the importance of aligning supplementation with national schedules and verifying current guidelines.
Identify operational approaches for supplementation in cases of poor adherence or specific deficiencies.
key_facts: Vitamin B12: Daily 5–10 µg or weekly 2000 µg for vegan children or breastfed infants of unsupplemented vegan mothers.

Iron prophylaxis: Biweekly IFA syrup (20 mg iron + 100 µg folic acid) for 6–59 months; weekly tablets (45 mg iron + 400 µg folic acid) for 5–10 years; weekly tablets (60 mg iron + 500 µg folic acid) for 10–19 years.
Vitamin A: First dose at 9 months (100,000 IU), followed by 200,000 IU every 6 months until 5 years (total 9 doses).
Vitamin D: Routine 400 IU/day in infancy; supplementation for older children in high-risk contexts (low sun exposure, dairy-poor diets).
Zinc in diarrhea: 10 mg/day (<6 months) or 20 mg/day (≥6 months) for 14 days alongside ORS.
National schedules and program-anchored doses should be verified regularly to ensure compliance with updated guidelines.
reference_quote: Align with AMB/I-NIPI schedules. Operational doses commonly used in India include 6–59 months biweekly IFA syrup 1 mL (20 mg elemental iron + 100 µg folic acid).
document_key_topics: Preventive supplementation in high-risk vegetarian diets
National health program alignment for supplementation
Specific nutrient guidelines for children and adolescents
